title: Symmetry discovery vs hard-negative mining for generative models (EqM)
research_question: Can a learned manifold-preserving operator, anchored to data (not the live field), discover a nonlinearly-hidden symmetry and fill held-out manifold regions? vs hard-negative mining and known-symmetry constraints.
scale: CPU toy (2D/16D rings). NOT publishable; filters/diagnostics only.

ladder_status.rung1_arithmetic: equivariance constraint perfect; hard-neg mining dead
ladder_status.rung2_ring_linear: known + discovered rotation both work (symmetry linear in observed)
ladder_status.rung5_oracle_latent_control: recipe collapses M->identity even with perfect latent (recipe bug exposed)
ladder_status.rung8_identity_exclusion: FAIL (M drifts to non-symmetry; closure can't anchor)
ladder_status.rung9_frozen_anchor: PARTIAL: frozen anchor FIXES field co-adaptation (T_onman 0.00->0.70) but distributional anchor under-constrains operator -> random on-manifold shuffle (shift_std ~95deg), arc not filled. FROZEN~=VISIBLE (no symmetry learned).
ladder_status.rung10_frozen_anchor_coherent: PARTIAL SUCCESS (best yet): single latent matrix fixes coherence (shift_std 95->25), recovers real rotation (M_angle ~-39deg), recall floor0.002->0.025 (12x floor, ~37% of ORACLE 0.068). All 3 blockers (identity/co-adaptation/coherence) now individually fixed. Remaining gap = single-generator vs full-group COVERAGE + imperfect latent + seed variance.
ladder_status.rung11_orbit_augmentation: Coverage hypothesis REJECTED: orbit aug == single aug (recall 0.024 both). Gap to ORACLE is NOT coverage but operator PRECISION (discovered M is fuzzy/seed-variable rotation, shift_std 25, M_off 1.04). FINAL: discovery possible-in-principle but imprecise -> below known-symmetry quality.

key_findings: Hard-negative mining (v10 mechanism) installs no missing manifold structure in any rung.
Symmetry constraints generalize ONLY when the symmetry/group is known.
Unsupervised discovery blocked by: (1) identity attractor, (2) field co-adaptation [FIXED rung9 frozen anchor], (3) operator coherence under distributional anchoring [OPEN].
Methodology: positive+negative controls in every run isolated each root cause (ORACLE/ORACLE_LATENT/FIELD_CLOSURE/VISIBLE_ANCHOR).

next_action.reason: Ladder answered the question across 11 rungs. 4 blockers (identity/co-adaptation/coherence/precision); first 3 fixed, precision open but only incremental tuning remains.
next_action.hint: Open lever (NOT chased, rabbit-hole risk): operator precision via orthogonality reg on M / tighter latent / sharper anchor. Bottom line: mining dead, KNOWN symmetry best, unsupervised discovery partial+imprecise. For diff-EqM: inject known symmetries, don't build unsupervised discovery into paper critical path.

completed_runs.result: PARTIAL SUCCESS (coherence fixed, partial arc fill)

events.detail: Project created from diff-EqM symmetry-toys investigation (rungs 1-9). Houses ladder scripts, findings, control methodology.
Coherent single-matrix operator + frozen anchor -> partial discovery success; 3-layer blocker resolved in principle.
Orbit aug rejected coverage hypothesis; gap is operator precision. Ladder concluded: discovery partial+imprecise, known-symmetry remains best lever.

provenance.informs: diff-EqM v10 direction (prefer known-symmetry constraints over hard-negative mining)
